latest.title: Bats don't use sonar like we think
latest.script_key: bats don t just scream to navigate their ears and mouth move like tiny radar dishes watch this one twice those hand flaps aren t flailing they re aiming sound beams that s why they can weave through branches blindfolded did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Monkeys wash food when grit gets annoying
latest.script_key: monkeys can change habits to solve small problems watch the hands near the food because some monkeys learn useful routines from their group washing or handling food differently can remove grit and make eating easier the habit spreads because it works which primate trick should come next

latest.title: Seagulls survive winter in the mountains
latest.script_key: seagulls soaring over snow covered peaks isn t just pretty those wings don t just glide they trap heat like a down jacket because their feathers puff up more than any city bird s that s why you ll see them on glaciers at dawn still flapping while the valley below freezes did you think seagulls only followed boats

latest.title: Storm clouds reveal a storm's heat engine
latest.script_key: storm clouds show where a storm is feeding watch the spiral shape because warm ocean air rises near the center and releases heat as clouds build rotation organizes that energy into bands from above the storm is not random it is an engine which satellite clue next

latest.title: Mountains breathe through hidden cracks
latest.script_key: these peaks exhale watch the snow vanish into thin air right where the rock splits that crack isn t just a fracture it s a breathing vent cold air rushes downhill at night pulling heat out of the mountain like a lung that s why glaciers shrink faster than we thought we forgot the mountain s doing the work

latest.title: Trees record wet years inside their rings
latest.script_key: trees keep weather history in their rings watch the trunk pattern because each growing season leaves a new layer wide rings often mean easier growth while thin rings can mark stress that is why an old tree can become a climate diary which hidden record should come next

latest.title: Trees stretch sideways not straight up
latest.script_key: trees don t grow straight up like we all assume watch this those roots snaking sideways are the real trunks that s why big trees don t topple in storms because their strength is underground the trunk is just the delivery system did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: butterfly feet taste flowers first
latest.script_key: this butterfly s feet just tasted lunch watch closely those tiny feet press and taste flowers before the wings even flutter that s why it lands on the wrong one skips the sweet stuff fast would you trust a tongue that tastes with its feet

latest.title: Bears smell food from miles away
latest.script_key: bears can smell a meal long before seeing it watch the nose first because smell drives many bear decisions before the eyes do that huge scent map helps them find food avoid danger and read who crossed the area earlier would you trust your nose that much

latest.title: Huskies aren't just cute they're built to run
latest.script_key: huskies weren t bred for looks they re endurance machines those thick coats they trap heat so well they don t pant like other dogs until it s 75 f outside that s why they can pull sleds for hours without overheating no wonder they ve got that wolfy stare they re built to outlast the cold

latest.title: Wolfs detect changes with their eyes
latest.script_key: wolfs detect changes with their eyes watch the eyes first because that small signal shows where attention shifts before the body commits the clip looks simple but timing turns it into survival information which wolf clue should we decode next

latest.title: Dogs read the world through scent maps

latest.title: Snakes smell the air with their tongues

latest.title: Free-range chickens aren’t just scratching around
latest.script_key: watch their feet they re not scratching for food they re killing roosters teach chicks to hunt insects like they re learning a game that s why free range flocks grow faster you ever seen a chicken tear apart a grasshopper it s messy who taught them to do that

latest.title: Cats use whiskers like built-in rulers

latest.title: Dolphins can call each other with signature whistles
latest.script_key: dolphins can call each other with signature whistles watch the group spacing because dolphins use sound to keep track of individuals in open water a signature whistle works like a personal label helping the pod reconnect when visibility drops ocean social life is loud which sound should we decode next
